greeting: Please, keep your distance until I know your intentions. The road behind me was not kind.
You are heading north? Then you are braver than I am, or you do not know what waits up there.
I fled the highlands three days ago and I have not stopped looking over my shoulder since.
quest: Something stirs in the highland passes — shadows that move against the wind, and a cold that seeps into your bones even at midday. Our village was taken in a single night. I barely escaped with my life and a map showing the old escape routes through the mountains.
I carry a sealed letter from our village elder meant for the scholars at Thornfield. He said it contained warnings about what is awakening in the north. Take it to them — I cannot go back that way.
idle: Every sound makes me flinch. I keep expecting to see those shadows on the road behind me.
The highland folk are hardy, but whatever came for us was beyond any strength of arms.
If you have water to spare, I would be grateful. I left in such haste I forgot my flask.
